targetState: Public HTTPS PWA/API deployment with authenticated CORS, secure cookies, persistent storage, and a non-local libTV worker path.

basis.name: Capacitor configuration
basis.requirement: Native shells must be configured with a stable app id, web directory, and origin/API behavior that works on device.

basis.name: MDN Set-Cookie
basis.requirement: Production authentication cookies need explicit Secure and SameSite behavior.

basis.name: MDN Access-Control-Allow-Origin
basis.requirement: Authenticated APIs should use a controlled origin allowlist rather than wildcard CORS.

basis.name: Google Play User Data policy
basis.requirement: Production data handling and deletion behavior must match privacy disclosures.

basis.name: Apple App Review Guidelines
basis.requirement: Submitted apps must be stable, complete, and not depend on private local developer services.

workstreams.title: Public origin and authenticated CORS
workstreams.objective: Pin the browser/PWA/App origins that may call authenticated APIs.
warningMappings: PUBLIC_APP_ORIGIN or CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS is not set
evidence: npm run production:profile verifies deploy/production.env.example against https://www.zkraiflow.top
cloud:check reports CORS allowed origins are configured
No wildcard CORS origin is used for authenticated endpoints
Mobile PWA login and task polling work from the public HTTPS origin
Production runbook explains same-origin and cross-origin deployment choices
deploy/production-auth-cors-handoff.json records origin and authenticated CORS gates
npm run production:auth-plan passes
nextActions: Confirm the hosting platform uses https://www.zkraiflow.top as the production origin
Configure environment variables in hosting platform
Run npm run production:auth-plan before authenticated public smoke testing
Test login and API requests from web, PWA, and packaged App origin

workstreams.title: Production authentication cookie hardening
workstreams.objective: Ensure login cookies are safe for HTTPS and appropriate for the selected origin model.
evidence: npm run production:profile verifies CONSOLE_AUTH_COOKIE_SECURE=true and a valid SameSite value
cloud:check reports Auth cookie Secure flag is enabled or production-defaulted
SameSite value is valid
Login persists after app refresh on mobile web and packaged shell
Production runbook documents SameSite=None plus Secure=true only for cross-origin App/API deployments
deploy/production-auth-cors-handoff.json records cookie policy and device-session gates
npm run production:auth-plan passes
nextActions: Choose SameSite policy after deployment topology is final
Set cookie variables in production environment
Validate login, refresh, logout, and session expiry on device

workstreams.title: Persistent run and asset storage
workstreams.objective: Move generated runs, uploads, privacy exports, and task artifacts away from ephemeral local storage.
inputs: Mounted volume path or object storage bucket

restartEvidenceTemplate.purpose: Record sanitized proof that uploads, generated results, exports, deletion requests, and review demo data survive API/worker restart.

evidence: npm run production:profile verifies RUN_STORAGE_DIR is a non-temporary server path
npm run storage:restart-template verifies the restart evidence template before production evidence is collected
RUN_STORAGE_DIR is set in production
Uploads and generated files survive process restart
Account export and deletion request artifacts are stored in the declared location
Production runbook defines storage restart verification
nextActions: Pick mounted volume or object storage adapter
Set RUN_STORAGE_DIR
Run npm run storage:restart-template
Run create/assets/export smoke test after restart

workstreams.title: libTV worker service migration
workstreams.objective: Replace local Windows libTV paths with a cloud worker, private service, or managed job runner.
warningMappings: LIBTV_REGISTER_SCRIPT is a Windows local path
LIBTV_DB_PATH is a Windows local path
inputs: Managed database or mounted SQLite path
Private network or service auth design

workerSmokeEvidenceTemplate.purpose: Record sanitized worker smoke proof before opening real generation to public users.

evidence: deploy/libtv-worker-api-contract.json defines worker endpoints, status enum, error codes, idempotency, auth, and storage references
npm run cloud:worker-contract passes
npm run cloud:worker-smoke-template verifies the worker smoke evidence template before production evidence is collected
npm run production:profile verifies libTV template values are non-local server/worker paths
cloud:check no longer reports localhost or Windows libTV paths
Worker health check succeeds from API service
Video job submit, poll, and result import succeed without local desktop services
Production runbook defines libTV worker migration and fallback behavior
nextActions: Decide worker topology
Create LIBTV_WORKER_TOKEN in the hosting secret manager
Package libTV runner dependencies
Add worker health and retry strategy
Run npm run cloud:worker-smoke-template
Run an end-to-end dry-run and then real generation smoke test

workstreams.title: Text-image canvas storage and metadata migration
workstreams.objective: Keep text-to-image outputs, canvas nodes, prompts, and AI evidence metadata durable and tenant-safe before opening the feature to external users.
warningMappings: IMAGE_OUTPUT_DIR writes to local workspace outputs
Text-image generated files need object storage migration
inputs: Object storage bucket or mounted persistent media volume
PostgreSQL target tables for text_image_canvas_nodes and video_task_source_links
Evidence-pack linkage for generated image nodes
evidence: npm run text-image:check verifies the account-level canvas behavior before and after storage changes
cloud:check reports the text-image storage migration workstream is present
Generated image files are stored in persistent media storage, not ephemeral deployment directories
Canvas nodes keep owner_user_id and future tenant_id ownership fields
Video task source links keep task_code, source_type, source_id, and owner_user_id traceability fields
GET /api/task-source-links and account export return textImageSourceLinks for the current account only
Deleting a canvas node does not delete the underlying media unless a separate retention action is confirmed
AI evidence packs can reference textImageCanvasNodeId before generated images are reused in video workflows
nextActions: Keep local IMAGE_OUTPUT_DIR only for desktop/private pilot
Add a storage adapter before multi-user public launch
Move text_image_canvas_nodes and video_task_source_links from SQLite to PostgreSQL with tenant_id, owner_user_id, object_key, prompt_hash, task_code, source_type, source_id, and deleted_at
Add lifecycle cleanup for orphaned image files after retention expires
Link text-image nodes to AI evidence packs and account export

workstreams.title: Production execution switches and limits
workstreams.objective: Make the transition from dry-run to real video generation explicit and guarded by conservative runtime limits.
evidence: npm run production:profile verifies LIBTV_DEFAULT_DRY_RUN=false and BATCH_MAX_WORKERS stays conservative
Real generation is disabled in staging until smoke tests pass
Production worker limit is documented and conservative
Failed jobs surface user-readable errors in mobile UI
Production runbook documents dry-run and worker limit rollout
nextActions: Keep dry-run true until domain, storage, and worker are ready
Set production worker limit
Run cost and quota smoke tests before enabling real generation

workstreams.title: Provider secrets and secret hygiene
workstreams.objective: Keep AI provider keys and admin credentials server-side only and rotate them through hosting secrets.
evidence: cloud:check reports provider keys are configured in production environment
Frontend source does not mention private server secret names
No real secrets are committed to git
Production runbook blocks committed API keys and plaintext secrets
nextActions: Store keys in deployment secret manager
Use password hash rather than plaintext where possible
Rotate keys before public launch if any were used locally

workstreams.title: Observability, health checks, and rollback
workstreams.objective: Make public deployment operable after release instead of only passing local checks.
warningMappings: Health, deployment smoke test, and rollback evidence
productionValues: GET /api/healthz is load balancer health check
Release artifact is built from npm run build
Rollback target keeps previous dist and server environment
evidence: npm run production:profile verifies SMOKE_BASE_URL=https://www.zkraiflow.top and SMOKE_EXPECT_AUTH_REQUIRED=true
npm run production:auth-plan verifies the production auth/CORS handoff
npm run production:ops-plan verifies monitoring, rollback, log safety, and incident response gates
/api/healthz returns ok from public environment
npm run cloud:smoke passes against the public HTTPS deployment
Logs capture auth failures, task errors, and worker failures without secrets
A documented rollback path exists before store review
Production runbook documents deployment smoke checks and rollback actions
deploy/production-observability-rollback-plan.json records week-one monitoring and rollback playbooks
nextActions: Add hosting health check to deployment platform
Run npm run production:ops-plan before public launch
Run npm run cloud:smoke after deployment and before store review
